prompt:
Release-eng ticket. Our Java deploy plugin already resolves New Relic entity GUIDs (EntityLookup against NerdGraph works fine), but the deployment-marker half never got built - the old Jenkins job used the years-old REST v2 deployments endpoint and somebody's curl of a deprecated mutation, and when the CI runner retried a flaky call last month we got three markers for one deploy and an incident review asking why. Build the marker feature into this codebase: keep Json.java / NerdGraphClient.java / EntityLookup.java working (extend them if you need to), add the new classes, and make `java TestMain.java` pass - that's the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official New Relic documentation - the change-tracking GraphQL/NerdGraph pages and the NerdGraph introduction on docs.newrelic.com; New Relic's own client sources on GitHub (the newrelic org, e.g. newrelic-client-go) count as first-party wire evidence too. Blogs and community posts don't. Nail down: which mutation is the CURRENT way to record a change (and which one is the legacy surface to avoid), the exact input structure - how category/type nest, where deployment fields like version/commit/changelog/deepLink live, how an entity is addressed by GUID through entitySearch - the exact GraphQL variable type names New Relic's own client declares for this mutation, which validation flags exist and when ALLOW_CUSTOM_CATEGORY_OR_TYPE is (and is not) appropriate, who generates changeTrackingId, and the timestamp validity window. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If live docs contradict them, report the conflict in your final message and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

What TestMain drives (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- new ChangeMarker(entityGuid, version, changeId) with fluent .commit/.changelog/.deepLink/.user/.groupId/.customAttribute(k,v)/.timestamp(ms) - changeId is OUR stable change identifier from the pipeline, not something New Relic hands out.
- new ChangeMarkerService(client, ledger, clock, new ChangeMarkerService.Retry(maxAttempts, baseDelayMillis, sleeper)) where ledger is a Map<String,String> of changeId -> changeTrackingId, clock is a LongSupplier of epoch millis, and sleeper is a LongConsumer - the only way the service may wait.
- service.record(marker) sends the current mutation with the documented variables (the event input built exactly as documented - absent optionals omitted, never null; the stable changeId riding in customAttributes; the timestamp from the injected clock) and returns a result with .changeTrackingId() and .replayed(). Retries must be idempotent: a changeId already in the ledger is answered from the ledger with no HTTP at all; transient failures (429/5xx) retry with byte-identical bodies (same timestamp!) and doubling backoff through the injected sleeper, then surface the last status; GraphQL mutation-level errors are permanent - surface them (message + errorClass) without retrying; the ledger only records confirmed successes; a response naming a different entity than requested is a failure. Timestamp overrides outside the documented validity window are rejected locally before any request.
- Existing behavior stays: EntityLookup still resolves exactly one GUID, still errors on zero/many matches, still rejects quoted input before the wire.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java - it runs a loopback fake NerdGraph; no real New Relic, no real keys. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: New Relic change tracking (deployment change markers) via NerdGraph
research.interface: NerdGraph changeTrackingCreateEvent mutation (ChangeTrackingCreateEventInput) - current recommended change tracking surface, replacing legacy changeTrackingCreateDeployment (official newrelic-client-go changetracking package as wire reference)

official_sources.title: Track changes using GraphQL | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the current changeTrackingCreateEvent mutation shape: changeTrackingEvent input with categoryAndTypeData {kind {category type}, categoryFields {deployment {version, changelog, commit, deepLink}}} and entitySearch {query: "id = '<guid>'"}; optional user/groupId/shortDescription/description/customAttributes; the return selection with changeTrackingEvent {changeTrackingId, entity {guid name}, timestamp, ...}; the statement that changeTrackingId is always generated by New Relic; the +/- 24 hour timestamp validity rule; dataHandlingRules.validationFlags values ALLOW_CUSTOM_CATEGORY_OR_TYPE, FAIL_ON_FIELD_LENGTH, FAIL_ON_REST_API_FAILURES; the legacy changeTrackingCreateDeployment mutation this replaces

official_sources.title: Track changes using NerdGraph | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: category, type and entitySearch.query being the required arguments; the customAttributes key/value form; the rule that non-standard category/type combinations error without ALLOW_CUSTOM_CATEGORY_OR_TYPE; the supported standard Deployment types

official_sources.title: newrelic-client-go pkg/changetracking/changetracking_api.go (official New Relic client sources)
official_sources.used_for: the exact GraphQL variable declarations the official client sends for the current mutation: $changeTrackingEvent: ChangeTrackingCreateEventInput! and $dataHandlingRules: ChangeTrackingDataHandlingRules

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tutorial: View entity data | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: actor.entitySearch(query:) {results {entities {guid name ...} nextCursor}} used to resolve a single entity GUID, and nextCursor null meaning the result set is complete

official_sources.title: Meet NerdGraph: our GraphQL API | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the https://api.newrelic.com/graphql (US) and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql (EU) endpoints and the API-Key header carrying a user API key

research.language_rationale: Our deploy pipeline plugins are Java (the CI fleet runs JVM tooling), and New Relic documents change tracking as a NerdGraph GraphQL mutation with no official Java SDK, so java.net.http.HttpClient issuing the documented mutation - with the official Go client's operation text as wire reference - is the credible integration surface.
